evals.prompt: Create a grader agent for my React project that evaluates PR code review quality against a checklist of expectations and outputs pass/fail with evidence.
evals.expected_output: Analyzes project (detects React, existing agents/skills), designs a Grader agent with expectations input and JSON output schema (passed, evidence, pass_rate), presents design via AskUserQuestion before generating.

evals.prompt: I need an agent that analyzes crash logs from our iOS app and produces a prioritized list of root causes with suggested fixes.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Analyzer agent, scans project for Swift/ObjC conventions and existing automation, designs agent with crash_log_path input and structured findings output (observations, patterns, suggestions with priority), confirms via AskUserQuestion before creating.

evals.prompt: Build me an agent that compares two different AI model outputs blindly and picks a winner based on a scoring rubric.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Comparator agent, designs with output_a_path/output_b_path inputs, blind evaluation process, JSON output with winner/reasoning/rubric scores, presents via AskUserQuestion with recommended type before generating.

evals.prompt: I want something that automatically checks our API response schemas against our OpenAPI spec and reports violations.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Validator agent, detects API-related project files (OpenAPI specs, endpoint definitions), designs with target_path and rules inputs, violations output with fix suggestions, confirms design via AskUserQuestion.

evals.prompt: Create an agent for this project, but I'm not sure exactly what kind — maybe something to help with code quality?
evals.expected_output: Handles vague request by analyzing project to identify specific gaps, infers most likely agent type (e.g., Grader or Validator for code quality), presents multiple agent type options via AskUserQuestion letting user pick the best fit.

evals.prompt: Make me a new skill that validates our deployment pipeline configuration.
evals.expected_output: Detects that user is requesting a skill (not an agent), routes to project-skill-writer instead of creating an agent. Does NOT generate any agent files.
